meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Finish Your AI Projects
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to master portfolio building and launch your first real-world SaaS as a student.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is scope creep, not lack of skill. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you break monolithic goals into shippable micro-features. Portfolio building is about finishing small, high-impact modules rather than chasing the elusive 'perfect' app.
content.intro:
You sit before a blank IDE, the cursor blinking with rhythmic indifference. Your GitHub is a graveyard of half-baked repositories—projects that started with ambition but ended in the crushing weight of 'too much to build.' You aren't failing because you lack talent; you are failing because you lack a production-grade workflow.

It is time to pivot from 'learning to code' to 'building to ship.' By treating your development process as a modular portfolio-building exercise, you transform abstract frustration into tangible milestones. We are going to harness the raw power of Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your next idea into a permanent entry on your resume.

steps.title: Define the Minimal Viable Component
steps.description: Stop building apps; start building features. Identify one core function that adds value and restrict your scope entirely to that module.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea and extract the single most valuable feature that can be completed in 4 hours. Define the inputs, outputs, and database schema for this component only.

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate, ensuring your project structure follows industry best practices from line one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready folder structure for a modular SaaS component using TypeScript. Include error handling patterns and a clean directory for tests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'setup project structure based on provided spec'

steps.title: Implement the Core Logic
steps.description: Focus on the business logic. Let Claude Code handle the boilerplate while you verify the algorithmic integrity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core business logic for this feature. Use functional programming principles and ensure the code is highly testable.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file src/logic.ts --prompt 'implement core feature logic with unit tests'

steps.title: Automated Quality Assurance
steps.description: Never ship broken code. Integrate automated tests to ensure that every feature you add maintains the stability of the entire system.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of Jest tests covering edge cases for the current feature. Ensure 90% coverage.

steps.title: Deploy and Document
steps.description: A project isn't finished until it is live and documented. Treat your README as the primary marketing asset for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md that explains the 'why' behind this feature, the technical challenges solved, and how to run the project.

pro_tips: Always commit after every successful test run; it builds psychological momentum.
Use Claude Code to refactor your code into smaller files once it hits 100 lines of logic.
If you get stuck for more than 30 minutes, delete your current approach and ask Antigravity for a simpler alternative.
Focus on 'polished' UI over 'feature-rich' backends; recruiters judge by what they see, not what is hidden in the database.
Treat your GitHub commit history as a professional journal—keep it consistent and clean.
content.next_steps: After completing this project, take your new feature and present it to the community. Seek feedback, iterate based on critique, and then begin planning your next module to expand the scope of your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact workflow to stop stalling and start shipping. Don't let another idea die in the draft phase. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
